Protein names: Juvenile hormone acid O-methyltransferase (EC 2.1.1.325) (Juvenile hormone acid methyltransferase) (BmJHAMT)
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the methyltransferase superfamily. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=(2E,6E)-farnesoate + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = methyl (2E,6E)-farnesoate + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:43700, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:80535, ChEBI:CHEBI:83276; EC=2.1.1.325; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:14530389}; CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=juvenile hormone III carboxylate + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = juvenile hormone III + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:43720, ChEBI:CHEBI:27493, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:83274; EC=2.1.1.325; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:14530389};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: O-methyltransferase that transfers a methyl group from S-adenosyl-L-methionine (SAM) to the carboxyl group of juvenile hormone acids to produce active juvenile hormones in the corpora allata, the last step during juvenile hormone biosynthesis (PubMed:14530389). Also able to methylate farnesoate to methyl farnesoate (PubMed:14530389). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:14530389}.
